steps.label: Prepare procedure based on technical competence

constraints.text: This procedure was prepared by persons deemed technically competent by LSASD management

steps.label: Place official procedure copy on LAN

constraints.text: The official copy of this procedure resides on the LSASD Local Area Network (LAN)
The Quality Assurance Manager is responsible for ensuring the most recent version of the procedure is placed on the LAN

steps.label: Maintain records of review prior to issuance

constraints.text: maintaining records of review conducted prior to its issuance

steps.label: Use standard procedures during field investigations
Conduct periodic review of procedures

constraints.text: LSASD procedures are subject to periodic review
LSASD procedures undergoing periodic review that are updated with minor modifications are not subject to the validation process described in Section 4

steps.label: Revise procedures to ensure suitability and conformance

constraints.text: where necessary, may be revised to ensure continuing suitability and conformance with applicable industry standards and requirements

steps.label: Evaluate new standard procedures for equivalence

constraints.text: to the extent necessary to ensure the result provided is equivalent to a currently used procedure or consistent results are achieved
New standard procedures adopted for use by the FSB will not be subject to an extensive validation as described in Section 4
New standard procedures may be adopted for use by LSASD field investigators

steps.label: Inform customer in writing of non-standard procedure use

constraints.text: in writing (email or memo)
When it is necessary to use a non-standard sampling or measurement procedure, the project leader will

steps.label: Document customer agreement with non-standard procedure
Document customer request and need for non-standard procedure
steps.action_object: customer request and need in project file

constraints.text: in the project file the customer’s request and the need for using a non-standard procedure (i.e., description of the project request and why it required the use of a non-standard procedure)

steps.label: Fully validate non-standard procedure

constraints.text: according to Section 4 of this procedure
Validation will be as extensive as is necessary to meet the needs of the given application

steps.label: Identify non-standard results outside quality system scope
steps.action_object: results outside quality system scope in report

constraints.text: If the non-standard procedure is not adopted into the scope of the FSB quality system prior to use, any results generated by the procedure must be clearly identified as being outside the scope of the quality system in the field investigation report

steps.label: Include non-standard procedure in quality system scope
steps.action_object: non-standard procedure in quality system scope

constraints.text: If FSB choose to include the non-standard procedure in the scope of their quality system, this will be done in accordance with the LSASD Operating Procedure for Document Control (LSASDPROC-1000, most recent version)

steps.label: Plan procedure development activity

constraints.text: When it is necessary for FSB to develop procedures for their own use, it will be a planned activity

steps.label: Designate qualified personnel to develop procedure

constraints.text: Management will designate qualified personnel to develop the procedure

steps.label: Ensure adequate resources for procedure development

constraints.text: Management will also ensure adequate resources (time, equipment, funding, personnel, etc.) are available to aid in the development process

steps.label: Designate team leader for procedure development

constraints.text: If a team of staff members is tasked with the procedure development, management will designate an individual to serve as the team leader
This individual will be responsible for facilitating the development process

steps.label: Review relevant literature for procedure development

constraints.text: Review of relevant literature (manufacturers' documentation, scientific publications, etc.)

steps.label: Develop operating procedure
Designate validation team members

constraints.text: LSASD FSB management will designate members of the validation team
Members may consist of subject matter experts (SMEs) and representatives from both FSB sections, as appropriate

steps.label: Ensure adequate resources for validation

constraints.text: ensure adequate resources are available to complete the validation

steps.label: Designate validation team leader

constraints.text: Management will designate one team member as the team leader
The team leader will serve as the point of contact for the team and facilitate the validation process

steps.label: Develop validation plan

constraints.text: a validation plan that should address, at a minimum, the objective of the validation, acceptance criteria, the level of testing necessary and peer review

steps.label: Update validation plan as necessary

constraints.text: as necessary, during the validation process

steps.label: Develop field test or evaluation

constraints.text: The validation team will develop an appropriate field test or evaluation for the operating procedure

steps.label: Conduct field test or evaluation

constraints.text: A member or members of the validation team will be present during the field test or evaluation

steps.label: Record conditions, performance, and practicality during field test
Assess field test results against acceptance criteria

constraints.text: using the acceptance criteria established by the team in the validation plan
After completion of the field test or evaluation

steps.label: Finalize operating procedure as written or with modifications

constraints.text: If the assessment indicates that the operating procedure meets the acceptance criteria, the validation team will finalize the operating procedure as written, or with modifications

steps.label: Return to earlier validation steps if criteria not met

constraints.text: If the acceptance criteria are not met, the team may continue the validation by returning to earlier steps in the validation process, as appropriate

steps.label: Document all validation decisions and actions

constraints.text: The validation team will document all decisions and actions

The following records will be generated during the validation process: Validation plan; Relevant literature; Draft and final operating procedures; Results of field tests and/or evaluations; Results of the assessment

steps.label: Maintain validation records during validation process

constraints.text: The validation team leader will be responsible for maintaining the records during the validation process

steps.label: Maintain validation records after validation completes

constraints.text: the records will be maintained by the Quality Assurance Manager

steps.label: Provide training on operating procedure implementation

constraints.text: After a standard or nonstandard procedure has been assessed and accepted for use
The subject matter expert will provide training to the appropriate staff

steps.label: Document competency for each assigned staff member

constraints.text: for each staff member who will be assigned to implement the operating procedure
in accordance with LSASD Operating Procedure for Training and Competency (LSASDPROC-1003, most recent version)

quality.manual_correction_summary: Source-to-gold review corrected C9, C11, C13, C14, C27-C29, C31-C34, C37, C40-C41, S33, and S35 without applying human sign-off. Scope, general validation attachment, and graph relations remain open for human adjudication.
open_questions: Human adjudication required: the declared span includes sections 1-5 although procedure.source.section says sections 3-5; decide whether S1-S3 belong in this procedure.
Human adjudication required: S1 and C1 describe past document preparation rather than an executable action.
Human adjudication required: rebuild relations for three alternative procedure categories and the validation retry path before using graph metrics.
Human adjudication required: decide the full attachment set for C46, which governs validation extent generally rather than only S11.

EDGE CASE (short standalone): full document is a single complete process (<2k words); annotated end-to-end per guidelines short-standalone rule.
[verbatim-grounding pass 2026-07-06, agent, pending human sign-off] Eight constraint texts re-grounded to contiguous verbatim source spans (C6, C7, C19, C24, C26, C31, C35, C13 extended with its i.e.-parenthetical; C41 narrowed to the referenced document title to remove full containment inside C40). Dropped C5 (Section 2.1 definition — guidelines 'What to drop: Definitions'), C12, C22, C30 (zero-information restatements of their own steps). Retyped per the guidelines decision sequence: C7 -> guard/may ('where necessary, may be revised'), C8 and C17 -> guard (exemption/when-trigger conditions), C29 -> role_assignment (team-member presence), C35 -> postcondition (generated records). Added missed constraints: C42 'After completion of the field test or evaluation' (precondition, S28), C43 facilitator responsibility (role_assignment, S17), C44 team composition (parameter/may, S20), C45 team-leader point of contact (role_assignment, S22), C46 'Validation will be as extensive as is necessary...' (parameter, S11), C47 'the project leader will' 3.2.1 role intro (role_assignment, S8-S10), C48 'New standard procedures may be adopted...' (guard/may, S7). 41 -> 44 constraints.
step:S4 zero_constraints adjudicated (definition dropped; usage step carries no separate condition); step:S9 zero_constraints adjudicated (covered via C47 attachment); step:S19 zero_constraints adjudicated; step:S27 zero_constraints adjudicated
[relations pass 2026-07-06, agent, pending human sign-off] Step-graph relations annotated by hand: NEXT = document-order procedural succession; fork NEXT edges plus ALTERNATIVE_TO only where the source states mutually exclusive branches (S12 outside-quality-system vs S13 adopt-into-quality-system per 3.2.2/3.2.3; S29 finalize vs S30 return-to-earlier-steps per 4.5.1; both rejoin at S31). Conditional in-step behaviour stays encoded as guard constraints. Loops (check-measurement repeat, per-vertical sampling) not modeled to keep the graph acyclic. NOTE: current Tier-B eval synthesizes a linear NEXT chain from steps-array order and does not read this field; relations are provided as explicit gold ordering/branching for graph consumers and export.
